patches.author: @benjaminn8
patches.summary: shopifyql funnel uses real ShopifyQL response columns, not hallucinated ones.
patches.reason: The novel-feature shopifyql funnel command originally referenced column names (e.g. funnel_step, step_count) that do not exist in Shopify's ShopifyQL response schema, producing empty results against live data. Replacing them with the actual schema fields makes the command return real funnel rows against a live store. This lives only in this CLI's hand-coded internal/cli/shopifyql.go and is novel-feature substance, not a generator template bug.
patches.validated_outcome: Funnel command returns real session/checkout/order counts against a live Shopify store.

patches.author: @benjaminn8
patches.summary: sync --since now uses UTC RFC3339 and binds the resulting timestamp into the GraphQL query variable so updated_at filtering actually runs.
patches.reason: Shopify GraphQL updated_at filtering requires the `query: $query` field arg on resource connections, with an RFC3339 UTC timestamp. The generated sync.go formats --since via .Format(time.RFC3339) on a local time.Time (which Shopify parses as UTC, producing a wrong-window cursor) and never binds sinceTS into the variables map at all (so even a correct timestamp does nothing). Fix applies .UTC() before .Format and adds the variables["query"] = "updated_at:>=" + sinceTS bind for every resource except fulfillment-orders, whose schema has no query arg. Generator template bug; cli-printing-press#2056 covers the UTC half; the dead-bind half is a related defect surfaced in this reprint.
patches.validated_outcome: shopify-pp-cli sync orders --since 7d returns only orders updated in the last 7 days against a live Shopify store, not the full table.

patches.author: @benjaminn8
patches.reason: Default --max-pages 10 multiplied by default page size 50 silently caps every sync to 500 rows. Full-store backfills appear successful but return partial data with no warning. Default 0 (unlimited) matches operator intent; users who need a cap pass a positive number explicitly. Generator template bug filed as cli-printing-press#2057, not yet absorbed.
patches.validated_outcome: shopify-pp-cli sync orders against a live store's full order history returns all rows, not the first 500.

patches.author: @benjaminn8
patches.summary: --query flag now wired into GraphQL variables on 5 list commands, including the matching $query argument declarations in queries.go.
patches.reason: Generated template emits a --query flag on orders/customers/products/inventory-items/abandoned-checkouts list commands but (a) the Go side never passes the value into the GraphQL variables map and (b) the corresponding GraphQL queries in internal/client/queries.go don't declare a $query: String variable or pass it into the connection's `query:` argument. Either half alone is a silent no-op. Fix is two-part: add the conditional variables["query"] = flagQuery bind in each list command, and add `$query: String` to the operation signature plus `query: $query` to the connection in queries.go. Fulfillment-orders list is intentionally left dead because Shopify's fulfillmentOrders GraphQL field has no query arg; that is API truth, not a template bug. Generator template bug filed as cli-printing-press#2058.
patches.validated_outcome: shopify-pp-cli orders list --query 'created_at:>2026-05-01' --first 5 returns only orders created after 2026-05-01 against a live store; without the fix the filter silently no-ops and returns the oldest 5 orders in the store.

patches.author: @benjaminn8
patches.summary: orders list no longer exposes --sort-key or --reverse; the v3.2.1 printing carried them via a now-superseded query/search/resolve patch.
patches.reason: v3.2.1 (printed by @cathrynlavery) carried a `orders-query-search-resolve` patch that wired --sort-key, --reverse, and several orders search helpers into the generated orders surface. This reprint replaces that approach with the generic --query flag wiring (patch list-query-flag-wiring), which covers the same filter use cases via Shopify's native search syntax and does not need bespoke sort flags. Automations relying on the old --sort-key / --reverse will see unknown-flag errors and must switch to --query 'sort_key:created_at-asc' or equivalent search-syntax expressions. Recording this here so future regen and reprint tooling treats the removal as intentional, not a regression.
patches.validated_outcome: shopify-pp-cli orders list --help no longer advertises --sort-key/--reverse; --query covers the same filter shapes.
